steps.title: Identify the right triangle setup
steps.content:
In right triangle $QRS$ with $QR\lt RS$, the side opposite the right angle is the hypotenuse $\overline{QS}$. The given leg of length $17$ is opposite angle $Q$. From angle $Q$:
$$\sin Q = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}} = \frac{17}{QS}$$

steps.title: Solve for $QS$
steps.content:
$$QS = \frac{17}{\sin Q}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
